channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Australian Miner Expands Zambia Uranium Resource Base as Exploration
item.description: LUSAKA – Australian uranium explorer Atomic Eagle has significantly expanded the mineralised footprint of its flagship Muntanga Uranium Project, reinforcing Zambia’s growing profile as a strategic destination for uranium investment amid rising global demand for nuclear energy. The company announced that recent exploration drilling at the Chisebuka Deposit has successfully extended the known uranium resource [...] The post Australian Miner Expands Zambia Uranium Resource Base as Exploration appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Beyond the runway: Noma Mokoni charts new direction after Miss Universe Zim pageant
item.description: Gift Moyo Zimbabwean fashion model and Miss Universe Zimbabwe 2026 Top 18 finalist Nomaqhikiza Mokoni has reaffirmed that her participation in the national pageant was a once-off experience, saying... (via Ebusiness Weekly) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Ebusiness Weekly

item.title: Nine killed as train ploughs into bus at Chiredzi railway crossing @nehandaradio
item.description: A devastating collision between a passenger bus and a goods train in Chiredzi has left nine people dead, including two children, and dozens more injured. The post Nine killed as train ploughs into bus at Chiredzi railway crossing appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Zimbabwe Raises Minimum Wage by 80% as Stable ZiG Era Reshapes Wage Policy
item.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe has approved a substantial increase in minimum wages, lifting the wage floor for workers in unclassified sectors by 80% and introducing a new graded pay structure for domestic workers, in a move that signals growing confidence in the stability of the country’s ZiG currency regime and broader economic recovery. The wage adjustments, [...] The post Zimbabwe Raises Minimum Wage by 80% as Stable ZiG Era Reshapes Wage Policy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Zimbabwe inflation holds below 5% @bulawayo24
item.description: Zimbabwe’s annual inflation remained below five percent in May, reinforcing one of the country’s most significant economic stabilisation achievements in recent years despite a global oil price shock t... (via Bulawayo24 News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Wicknell Chivayo’s Sh375 billion Kenyan “Heist”: How the Tycoon Sneaked into JKIA’s Massive Airport Deal @wicknellchivayo
item.description: In a development that has ignited significant debate across Harare and Nairobi, controversial Zimbabwean businessman Wicknell Chivayo has emerged as a central figure in a colossal Sh375 billion (approximately US$2.9 billion) expansion project for Kenya’s Jomo Kenyatta International Airport (JKIA). This unexpected involvement has raised numerous questions, particularly given Chivayo’s well-documented history and flamboyant lifestyle [...] The post Wicknell Chivayo’s Sh375 billion Kenyan “Heist”: How the Tycoon Sneaked into JKIA’s Massive Airport Deal first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.dc:creator: Planet Rugby

item.title: Zimbabwe farmers surpass wheat planting record
item.description: Zimbabwe has surpassed last season’s winter wheat planting record, with more than 123,000 hectares already planted as of June 14, placing the country on track to achieve its national target of 125,000 hectares as the extended planting deadline comes to an end. The achievement marks another significant milestone in the country’s drive towards food security [...] The post Zimbabwe farmers surpass wheat planting record appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: World Cup official says twitch caused gesture resembling supremacist sign; FIFA says no breach
item.description: GENEVA — A World Cup video review official said his hand gesture resembling a white supremacist sign was caused by an involuntary twitch, and a FIFA committee concluded the Australian didn’t breach the sport’s disciplinary code. FIFA’s discrimination monitor had called for Shaun Evans to be removed from the tournament. Evans worked Germany’s opening 7-1 [...] The post World Cup official says twitch caused gesture resembling supremacist sign; FIFA says no breach appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Global shares are mostly higher
item.description: TOKYO — Global shares mostly advanced and Japan’s benchmark Nikkei 225 briefly topped 70,000 for the first time Tuesday before trimming early gains after the Bank of Japan raised its key interest rate to 1%. The quarter percentage point hike took the benchmark rate to its highest level in three decades. France’s CAC 40 jumped [...] The post Global shares are mostly higher appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Chiredzi Rail Disaster Claims Nine Lives, Leaves 25 Injured @263Chat
item.description: Nine people have been killed and 25 others injured following a collision between a passenger bus and a National Railways of Zimbabwe (NRZ) train at a level crossing in Chiredzi. The accident occurred at around 0700hrs on Tuesday when an NRZ train travelling from Triangle [...] The post Chiredzi Rail Disaster Claims Nine Lives, Leaves 25 Injured appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Mnangagwa’s Constitutional Amendment Power-Grab Scheme Rocked by Bribing Scandals @ZANUPF_Official
item.description: The debate surrounding Constitution Amendment Bill No. 3 (CAB3) has taken a fresh twist, with cash and vehicle donations linked to prominent businessmen and political figures triggering unease within Zanu PF and intensifying allegations of political inducement ahead of a crucial parliamentary vote. The controversy comes as Parliament prepares to vote on the proposed constitutional [...] The post Mnangagwa’s Constitutional Amendment Power-Grab Scheme Rocked by Bribing Scandals appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: NetOne’s OneMoney Expands Financial Access Through Nationwide Network @263Chat @NetOneCellular
item.description: NetOne’s mobile financial services platform, OneMoney says it is driving financial inclusion, creating employment opportunities and simplifying money transfers through an extensive distribution network that reaches communities across Zimbabwe. The mobile money platform has partnered with six banks AFC Commercial Bank, POSB, NBS Bank, BancABC, [...] The post NetOne’s OneMoney Expands Financial Access Through Nationwide Network appeared first on 263Chat. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: 263chat

item.title: Zimbabwe Central Bank cuts policy rate to 30% as inflation remains contained and foreign currency inflows surge @ReserveBankZIM
item.description: HARARE – The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has eased its monetary policy stance for the first time this year, cutting the Bank Policy Rate by five percentage points to 30%, citing subdued inflation, exchange rate stability and robust foreign currency inflows that have strengthened confidence in the economy. The decision was announced following a [...] The post Zimbabwe Central Bank cuts policy rate to 30% as inflation remains contained and foreign currency inflows surge appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo

item.title: Can the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX) Revitalize Zimbabwe’s Economy?
item.description: Can the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX) Revitalize Zimbabwe’s Economy? — via Foreign Policy Research Institute #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Foreign Policy Research Institute

item.title: Pardon Mapondera: gone but not forgotten at Venice
item.description: The fifth and final piece in our series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Pardon Mapondera, the artist whose practice the room finally arranges itself around, treats discarded plastic and glass jars not as ecological signal but as ritual matter, and produces, in the process, the most quietly devotional installation in the Biennale this year. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Gideon Gomo: from stone to bronze, the ancestral algorithm at Venice
item.description: The fourth in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Gideon Gomo’s first sustained body of bronze work, paired with discarded vehicle number plates and Zimbabwean springstone, is the move of a sculptor at full maturity rethinking the material vocabulary of his country’s most internationally legible art form. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
item.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist’s day-long procession through the city’s campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice. #Zimbabwe #Culture #ZimbabweCulture #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Franklyn Dzingai: family albums and the neural commons at Venice
item.description: The third in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Franklyn Dzingai’s reductive cardboard prints, built from the family album, the magazine clipping, the back-room studio photographs of mid-century Zimbabwe, turn out, on close looking, to be the most precisely engineered work in the room. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Felix Shumba: charcoal and the practice of disappearance at Venice
item.description: The second in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Felix Shumba’s six-metre charcoal wall and his Telephone Reader series anchor a body of work that argues drawing is not the act of capturing the seen, but the act of staying present to what is on its way out of sight. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Eva Raath: a slow archive of stitched memory at Venice
item.description: In the first of five long-form pieces on the Pavilion of Zimbabwe at #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Isn’t it lovely: a week in Venice with the Pavilion of Zimbabwe
item.description: A letter from Venice, written in the wake of the opening of Zimbabwe’s #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Valentine Eluwasi
